title: Time-Aware Memory
constraints: Every stored fact must include a timestamp
Relative time references (yesterday, last week) must resolve to actual dates
The agent must use current date/time to interpret temporal queries
Facts must be retrievable by time range
hints: Attach a datetime to every fact when it is stored, and include the current date/time in the system prompt
When the user says 'yesterday,' compute the actual date relative to now and use it for lookup
Build a retrieval function that can filter facts by time range (e.g., 'last 7 days') before injecting them into context

starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, SystemMessage

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: Facts stored without timestamps — "yesterday" is meaningless
memory = []

def store_fact(fact: str):
    memory.append(fact)

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    store_fact(f"User said: {user_input}")
    facts_block = "\n".join(memory)
    messages = [
        SystemMessage(content=f"You are a helpful assistant. Known facts:\n{facts_block}"),
        HumanMessage(content=user_input),
    ]
    response = llm.invoke(messages)
    return response.content

# Monday
print(chat("I had a meeting with the design team today."))

# Tuesday
print(chat("I submitted the Q3 report today."))

# Wednesday — "yesterday" should mean Tuesday, not Monday
print(chat("What did I do yesterday?"))

# Query by range
print(chat("What happened in the last 3 days?"))

print(f"\nStored memory: {memory}")
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: Facts stored without timestamps — "yesterday" is meaningless
memory = []

def store_fact(fact: str):
    memory.append(fact)

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    store_fact(f"User said: {user_input}")
    facts_block = "\n".join(memory)
    agent = Agent(
        role="Helpful Assistant",
        goal="Answer questions using known facts",
        backstory=f"You are a helpful assistant. Known facts:\n{facts_block}",
        llm=llm,
    )
    task = Task(description=user_input, expected_output="A helpful response", agent=agent)
    crew = Crew(agents=[agent], tasks=[task])
    return str(crew.kickoff())

# Monday
print(chat("I had a meeting with the design team today."))
# Tuesday
print(chat("I submitted the Q3 report today."))
# Wednesday — "yesterday" should mean Tuesday, not Monday
print(chat("What did I do yesterday?"))
# Query by range
print(chat("What happened in the last 3 days?"))
print(f"\nStored memory: {memory}")
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

# BUG: Facts stored without timestamps — "yesterday" is meaningless
memory = []

def store_fact(fact: str):
    memory.append(fact)

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    store_fact(f"User said: {user_input}")
    facts_block = "\n".join(memory)
    agent = Agent(
        name="Assistant",
        instructions=f"You are a helpful assistant. Known facts:\n{facts_block}",
    )
    result = Runner.run_sync(agent, user_input)
    return result.final_output

# Monday
print(chat("I had a meeting with the design team today."))
# Tuesday
print(chat("I submitted the Q3 report today."))
# Wednesday — "yesterday" should mean Tuesday, not Monday
print(chat("What did I do yesterday?"))
# Query by range
print(chat("What happened in the last 3 days?"))
print(f"\nStored memory: {memory}")

evaluationCriteria.label: Facts are timestamped
evaluationCriteria.description: Every stored fact includes the date/time when it was recorded

evaluationCriteria.label: Relative time references resolved
evaluationCriteria.description: Queries like 'yesterday' or 'last week' are correctly resolved to actual dates

evaluationCriteria.label: Time-range retrieval works
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent can filter and return facts from a specific time window

evaluationCriteria.label: Agent knows current date
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent uses the current date/time to interpret temporal references correctly
